title: pulicaria rajputanae
intro: Pulicaria rajputanae, also known as the Dhudli or Bhangra, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Pulicaria rajputanae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations like decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
